title: Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao

description: Pineapple Buns, or Bo Lo Bao, are a beloved Hong Kong bakery classic, known for their distinctive crispy, sweet crust resembling a pineapple, despite containing no actual pineapple. These soft, fluffy buns are a perfect treat for breakfast or an afternoon snack, offering a delightful contrast in textures. Enjoy the authentic taste of a Chinese bakery right in your own home with this easy-to-follow recipe.

ingredients.notes: room temperature, for the bun dough

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine bread flour, 30g sugar, yeast, and salt. Add lukewarm milk and the whole egg. Mix until a shaggy dough forms. Turn onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Incorporate the softened butter and knead for another 5 minutes until fully absorbed and the dough passes the windowpane test.

instructions.title: First Proof
instructions.text: Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 60-90 minutes, or until doubled in size. This is a crucial step for a fluffy bun interior.

instructions.title: Make the Pineapple Topping
instructions.text: While the dough is proofing, cream together 70g softened butter and 80g sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the egg yolk until well combined. Gradually add the all-purpose flour and milk powder (if using), mixing until just combined and a soft dough forms. Do not overmix. Wrap in plastic and chill in the refrigerator.

instructions.title: Divide and Shape the Buns
instructions.text: Once the dough has doubled, punch it down gently to release air. Divide the dough into 8 equal portions. Roll each portion into a smooth ball. On a lightly floured surface, roll out the chilled pineapple topping dough into a thin sheet. Cut out 8 circles large enough to cover the top of each bun.

instructions.title: Apply Topping and Second Proof
instructions.text: Carefully place a topping circle over each bun, pressing gently to adhere. You can use a knife to create a criss-cross pattern on the topping if desired, mimicking a pineapple. Place the topped buns on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between them. Cover loosely with plastic wrap and let rise in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.

instructions.title: Bake the Pineapple Buns
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Just before baking, gently brush the topping of each bun with the beaten whole egg for a golden sheen. Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and the buns are cooked through. Remove from oven and let cool slightly on a wire rack before serving.

tips: Ensure your butter for both dough and topping is at room temperature for smooth incorporation and consistent texture.
For an extra aromatic topping, you can substitute a small amount of all-purpose flour with custard powder.
Do not overmix the topping dough; a slightly crumbly texture before baking helps create the signature crispy crust. Serve warm for the best experience.
article:
The Pineapple Bun, or Bo Lo Bao, is a beloved staple in Chinese bakeries worldwide, particularly celebrated in Hong Kong. Despite its name, this iconic pastry contains no pineapple. Its moniker comes from the distinctive golden, crumbly topping that resembles the skin of a pineapple. Beneath this sweet, crunchy crust lies a soft, fluffy milk bread bun, creating a delightful contrast of textures and flavors that captivates anyone who takes a bite. Whether enjoyed for breakfast, as a snack with a cup of milk tea, or a comforting treat any time of day, the Pineapple Bun holds a special place in the hearts of many. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that magic at home, you're in the right place, as we'll guide you through the ultimate Pineapple Bun Recipe.

## What is Pineapple Bun Bo Lo Bao?

Bo Lo Bao, or Pineapple Bun, is a quintessential Hong Kong-style sweet bun. It's characterized by two distinct components: a rich, buttery, and slightly crumbly cookie-like crust on top, and a soft, airy, slightly sweet bread dough underneath. The "pineapple" part refers solely to the appearance of the topping, which, after baking, develops a cracked, golden-brown texture reminiscent of a pineapple's exterior. The topping is typically made from sugar, eggs, flour, and butter, creating a wonderfully sweet and crisp contrast to the tender bread. The bun itself is a classic milk bread dough, known for its incredibly soft crumb and elasticity, which makes it a joy to eat. While traditional Pineapple Buns are unfilled, modern variations often include fillings such as sweet custard, red bean paste, or even savory BBQ pork (char siu), adding another layer of flavor. This comprehensive Pineapple Bun Recipe will demystify the process, allowing you to bring a taste of authentic Chinese bakery goodness into your kitchen.

## Ingredients You Need

Mastering this delightful Pineapple Bun Recipe begins with gathering the right ingredients. Precision is key in baking, so measuring accurately will yield the best results.

For the Soft Milk Bread Dough:
*   300g (2 ½ cups) Bread Flour (high protein content helps with chewiness)
*   30g (2 ½ tbsp) Granulated Sugar
*   3g (½ tsp) Salt
*   5g (1 ½ tsp) Instant Dry Yeast
*   180ml (¾ cup) Warm Milk (around 105-115°F / 40-46°C)
*   1 Large Egg, room temperature
*   30g (2 tbsp) Unsalted Butter, softened

For the Pineapple Topping:
*   80g (⅓ cup + 1 tbsp) Unsalted Butter, softened
*   80g (⅓ cup + 1 tbsp) Granulated Sugar
*   1 Large Egg Yolk
*   130g (1 cup + 2 tbsp) All-Purpose Flour
*   5g (1 tsp) Milk Powder (optional, enhances flavor)
*   2g (½ tsp) Baking Powder (optional, for extra crispiness)

For Egg Wash:
*   1 Large Egg, beaten
*   1 tsp Water or Milk

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Now that you have all your ingredients, let's dive into the step-by-step instructions for our perfect Pineapple Bun Recipe.

1.  **Prepare the Bread Dough:** In a large mixing bowl, combine bread flour, sugar, salt, and instant yeast. Whisk to combine. Add the warm milk and egg. Mix until a shaggy dough forms.
2.  **Knead the Dough:** Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface or use a stand mixer with a dough hook. Knead for 5-7 minutes until the dough is smooth and elastic. If using a stand mixer, knead on medium-low speed.
3.  **Incorporate Butter:** Add the softened butter and continue kneading for another 5-10 minutes (or 8-12 minutes in a stand mixer) until the dough is very smooth, elastic, and passes the windowpane test (you can stretch a small piece thin enough to see light through it without tearing).
4.  **First Proof:** Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, turning once to coat. Cover with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 60-90 minutes, or until doubled in size.
5.  **Prepare the Pineapple Topping:** While the dough is proofing, make the topping. In a medium bowl, cream together the softened butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the egg yolk until fully incorporated. Sift in the all-purpose flour, milk powder (if using), and baking powder (if using). Mix until just combined and a crumbly dough forms. Do not overmix. Gather the dough into a ball, flatten slightly, wrap in plastic wrap, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
6.  **Divide and Shape Buns:** Once the dough has doubled, gently punch it down to release air. Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces (around 60-70g each). Shape each piece into a smooth ball. Place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving space between each.
7.  **Shape and Apply Topping:** Retrieve the chilled topping dough. Divide it into the same number of pieces as your buns. On a lightly floured surface or between two sheets of parchment paper, flatten each piece of topping dough into a thin circle, slightly larger than your bun. Carefully place one topping circle over each bun, gently pressing to adhere it, ensuring it covers at least two-thirds of the bun's surface. You can use a knife to score a criss-cross pattern on the topping for the classic pineapple look.
8.  **Second Proof:** Cover the shaped buns loosely with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let them rise in a warm place for another 30-45 minutes, or until visibly puffy.
9.  **Preheat Oven & Egg Wash:** Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) about 15 minutes before the second proof is complete. Gently brush the exposed bread part (not the topping) with the egg wash.
10. **Bake:** Bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the topping is golden brown and cracked, and the buns are cooked through. If the topping browns too quickly, you can loosely tent with foil.
11. **Cool:** Transfer the baked Pineapple Buns to a wire rack to cool slightly before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Following these pro tips will significantly elevate your results when tackling this Pineapple Bun Recipe.

*   **Room Temperature Ingredients:** Ensure your milk, egg, and butter for the dough are at room temperature. This helps them incorporate smoothly and promotes consistent fermentation.
*   **Don't Rush Proofing:** Proofing is crucial for soft, airy buns. A warm, humid environment (like a turned-off oven with a bowl of hot water inside) is ideal. Don't rush it; let the dough double in size.
*   **Achieve the Windowpane Test:** For truly soft and fluffy buns, knead the dough until it passes the windowpane test. This indicates excellent gluten development.
*   **Chill the Topping:** Chilling the pineapple topping makes it easier to handle and prevents it from becoming too sticky. It also helps it hold its shape and crack beautifully during baking.
*   **Handle Topping Gently:** When applying the topping, be gentle. You want it to adhere without deflating the proofed bun. If it's too sticky, lightly flour your hands or roll it between parchment paper.
*   **Scoring for Aesthetics:** The signature criss-cross pattern on the topping isn't just for looks; it helps the topping crack more uniformly. Use a sharp knife or a dough scraper.
*   **Egg Wash Only the Bun:** Apply egg wash *only* to the exposed bread parts, not the pineapple topping. This helps the bread brown nicely without making the topping too dark or interfering with its crispness.
*   **Don't Overbake:** Overbaking can dry out the buns. Keep an eye on them; once the topping is golden and cracked, and the buns sound hollow when tapped, they're likely done.

## Serving Suggestions

Pineapple Buns are incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in various ways. The most classic serving suggestion is simply warmed, perhaps with a generous slice of cold butter tucked inside the still-warm bun, creating a delightful contrast of temperatures and textures. This is famously known as "Bo Lo Yau" in Hong Kong. They pair perfectly with a cup of strong Hong Kong-style milk tea, coffee, or even a glass of milk for a comforting breakfast or afternoon tea. They also make an excellent light dessert after a meal. For a more substantial snack, you can serve them alongside some fresh fruit or a light salad.

## Variations and Substitutions

Once you've mastered the classic, feel free to experiment with this versatile Pineapple Bun Recipe.

*   **Fillings:** While traditional Pineapple Buns are unfilled, you can easily add a filling. Before the second proof, gently flatten each dough ball, place a spoonful of sweet custard, red bean paste, or even savory char siu (BBQ pork) filling in the center, then carefully seal the dough around it before applying the topping.
*   **Flour Types:** For a slightly chewier bun, you can increase the bread flour content. If you prefer a softer, cake-like texture, a small portion of cake flour can be substituted for some of the all-purpose flour in the topping.
*   **Dairy-Free Options:** For a dairy-free version, substitute plant-based milk (almond, soy, oat) for cow's milk and use a high-quality vegan butter substitute for both the dough and the topping.
*   **Flavor Enhancements for Topping:** Add a teaspoon of vanilla extract or a pinch of lemon zest to the pineapple topping mixture for an extra layer of aroma and flavor. A touch of almond extract can also be a delightful addition.
*   **Sugar Adjustments:** You can slightly reduce the sugar in the bread dough if you prefer a less sweet bun, as the topping is already quite sweet.
*   **Vegan Pineapple Buns:** To make the entire Pineapple Bun Recipe vegan, use plant-based milk and butter, and substitute the egg in the dough with a flax egg (1 tbsp ground flaxseed + 3 tbsp water, let sit for 5 minutes) or mashed banana. For the topping, use vegan butter and a combination of plant-based milk and a pinch of turmeric for color instead of egg yolk.

Armed with this detailed guide, you're ready to embark on your own Pineapple Bun Recipe journey. The aroma of freshly baked Bo Lo Bao filling your kitchen is an experience in itself, and the taste of your homemade, perfectly soft and crunchy buns will be incredibly rewarding. Happy baking!
